title: Rowing With Crayons
content:
I see children rowing with crayons
    across a park lagoon.

    They are sagging,
    they have just killed a blackbird playing:

    the lot of you,
    scribbling to school.

    Later on, I retrieved
    the pieces of paper, ink covered, from a field.
meaning:
Paul Cameron Brown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Rowing With Crayons"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
